info.title: Idea Campaign RPC (v1)
info.description:
JSON-RPC interface for the public idea campaign host surface, currently implemented by the TS idea-engine runtime. Contract schemas are referenced via $ref to sibling files in this directory.

Scope note: the search/eval runtime has been archived. The engine manages an idea portfolio: campaign lifecycle, node reads, externally computed belief posteriors (node.set_posterior), lifecycle transitions (node.set_lifecycle), independently produced grounding-audit records (node.set_grounding_audit), optimistic whole-card revisions (node.revise_card), scoped provenance corrections (node.rewrite_provenance), posterior-based ranking (rank.compute), and promotion (node.promote).

Ref resolution: All external schema $ref values are relative to this OpenRPC document's directory.

Idempotency semantics (MUST):
1) Side-effecting methods require idempotency_key.
2) Engine deduplicates by (method, campaign_id, idempotency_key). For campaign.init (no campaign_id yet), dedupe by (method, idempotency_key).
2b) If the same idempotency_key is reused with a different request payload, the engine MUST reject with schema_validation_failed (-32002) and MUST NOT execute the request. The error MUST include error.data.reason="idempotency_key_conflict" and MUST include error.data.idempotency_key + error.data.payload_hash.
2c) Payload identity MUST be machine-defined: compute payload_hash = sha256(JCS(params_without_idempotency_key)). JCS = RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme (deterministic object key order + number formatting). Engines SHOULD apply default-value filling (e.g., node.list.limit omitted → 50) before hashing to avoid false conflicts for semantically identical intents.
3) On duplicate, engine MUST return the same logical response as the first call (result or error), without repeating side-effects. The response MUST include idempotency metadata (idempotency_key + is_replay); is_replay MUST be true on duplicate hits and false on first execution. All other fields MUST match the first response. For non-deterministic methods, this MUST be implemented by storing and replaying the first response, not by re-execution.
4) Idempotency records MUST be retained for the campaign lifetime (or until an explicit campaign.delete is added).

Error data contract (MUST):
All error responses MUST include a machine-readable JSON-RPC error.data object. Minimum fields: {"reason": string, "details"?: object}.
- For code -32001 (budget_exhausted), error.data.reason MUST be "dimension_exhausted" and error.data.details.exhausted_dimensions MUST list one or more exhausted budget dimensions.
- For code -32002 (schema_validation_failed), error.data.reason MUST distinguish at least: schema_invalid, idempotency_key_conflict, activation_condition_required, activation_condition_unexpected, archived_reason_required, posterior_status_not_supported_by_coverage.
- For code -32017 (promotion_blocked), error.data.reason MUST be posterior_missing or node_not_admitted.
- For code -32018 (lifecycle_transition_invalid), error.data.reason MUST distinguish at least: illegal_transition, entry_precondition_failed, posterior_write_lifecycle_invalid, grounding_audit_write_lifecycle_invalid, idea_card_revision_lifecycle_invalid, unknown_lifecycle_state. error.data.details MUST carry, per reason: illegal_transition — current_state, requested_state, allowed_next; entry_precondition_failed — current_state, requested_state, requirement; posterior_write_lifecycle_invalid / grounding_audit_write_lifecycle_invalid / idea_card_revision_lifecycle_invalid — current_state, allowed_states; unknown_lifecycle_state — found, allowed_states plus a migration hint.
- For code -32019 (revision_conflict), error.data.reason MUST be stale_revision and details MUST carry expected_revision and current_revision.

params.name: abstract_problem_registry
params.description: Optional caller-provided abstract problem registry. There is no engine built-in registry. The engine MUST reject inputs that contain duplicate abstract_problem_type values with schema_validation_failed (-32002) and error.data.reason=schema_invalid. Used at reduction_audit time to validate reduction_report.abstract_problem.

methods.name: campaign.status
methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status.

methods.name: campaign.topup
methods.summary: Monotonic budget top-up for an existing campaign (pilot-then-scale).
methods.description: Monotonic, additive budget top-up. Permitted when campaign status is running|paused|early_stopped|exhausted. If status is exhausted, successful topup MUST transition campaign status to running only if the campaign is no longer budget-exhausted after applying the top-up; otherwise it remains exhausted. If status is early_stopped (policy halt, not budget), topup adds budget but does NOT change the status; the caller must explicitly campaign.resume to re-enter running. If the campaign is completed, the engine MUST reject the request with campaign_not_active. campaign.topup MUST NOT return budget_exhausted; insufficient topups are represented by a successful result whose campaign_status remains exhausted. Idempotency note: on replay, the returned campaign_status/budget_snapshot reflects the first-call snapshot; callers MUST use campaign.status for current budget.

methods.name: campaign.pause
methods.description: Side-effecting. Permitted when campaign status is running|early_stopped|exhausted (running|early_stopped|exhausted → paused). Otherwise campaign_not_active.

methods.name: campaign.resume
methods.description: Side-effecting. Accepted when campaign status is paused|early_stopped. When resuming from paused or early_stopped, the engine MUST verify that no BudgetEnvelope dimension has remaining <= 0; if any does, it MUST reject with budget_exhausted (-32001) without changing state. Transitions paused|early_stopped → running. If status is exhausted, the engine MUST reject with budget_exhausted (-32001) without changing state (caller should campaign.topup first). If campaign is completed, returns campaign_not_active (-32015).

methods.name: campaign.complete
methods.summary: Mark a campaign as completed (→ completed).
methods.description: Side-effecting. Permitted when status is running|paused|early_stopped|exhausted|completed. Transitions the campaign into a terminal completed state. If already completed, the engine MUST treat this as a no-op (but still idempotent, with transition.changed=false). Read-only methods remain available after completion.

methods.name: node.get
methods.summary: Fetch a single IdeaNode by node_id within a campaign scope.
methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status.

methods.name: node.list

params.name: limit
params.description: Page size. Defaults to 50 if omitted. Maximum 500.

methods.description: Read-only. Permitted in any campaign status. Results are always scoped to the given campaign; filters that do not match the campaign return empty results. If limit is omitted, the engine MUST treat it as 50.

methods.name: node.set_posterior
methods.summary: Record the externally computed belief-graph posterior for a node.

params.name: posterior

evidence_count.description: Number of independent observations in the belief graph backing this posterior (effective sample size).

gaia_package_ref.description: Optional URI of the belief-graph package (inside the external project) that produced this posterior.

status.description: Whether the posterior is current guidance, provisional, or stale after a later close-prior audit. status=current MUST be backed by scoring-eligible coverage in the same request (saturated, or coverage_incomplete with the explicit exploratory waiver); otherwise the write is rejected with schema_validation_failed/posterior_status_not_supported_by_coverage.

params.description: The posterior to record. The engine stamps posterior.updated_at itself.

params.name: literature_coverage

status.description: Close-prior literature coverage status used by ranking/allocation gates.

survey_ref.description: Portable reference to the literature_survey_v1 or equivalent close-prior survey.

close_prior_matrix_ref.description: Portable reference to the close-prior matrix used by the posterior report.

exploratory_allocation.description: Explicit waiver for exploratory allocation over coverage_incomplete literature.

params.description: The close-prior gate result that made this posterior admissible for portfolio surfaces.

methods.description:
Side-effecting. Writes the posterior computed by the external belief-graph tool onto the node, stamps posterior.updated_at, increments the node revision, and appends a set_posterior entry to the node mutation log. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active).

Lifecycle coupling (MUST): the write is legal only while the node's lifecycle_state is admission_review, admitted, or needs_refresh — a candidate node must first be moved to admission_review via node.set_lifecycle (declaring the review gives admission a logged, timestamped start), and admission_blocked / waiting_activation / archived nodes must be transitioned out before receiving posterior writes (else lifecycle_transition_invalid/posterior_write_lifecycle_invalid, -32018). After a legal write the engine derives the lifecycle itself: resolved posterior status current -> admitted; provisional or stale -> needs_refresh. When that derivation changes the state, lifecycle_reason is set to "posterior_status=<status>". Only the engine mutates lifecycle on posterior writes, so the state cannot drift from the stored posterior.

methods.name: node.set_lifecycle
methods.summary: Move a node through the enforced idea lifecycle state machine.

params.name: lifecycle_state
params.description: Target state. The transition must be legal from the node's current state AND satisfy the target's entry precondition (see method description).

params.name: activation_condition
params.description: Required when lifecycle_state is waiting_activation (the external condition to become actionable) or admission_blocked (the missing evidence that must be produced; kind is typically required_evidence) — activation_condition_required otherwise; MUST be absent for every other state (activation_condition_unexpected).

params.name: reason
params.description: Human-readable reason for the transition. REQUIRED (non-empty) when lifecycle_state=archived; optional otherwise. Stored on the node as lifecycle_reason and recorded in the node mutation log.

methods.description:
Side-effecting. Moves the node through the enforced lifecycle state machine, increments the node revision, records the reason as lifecycle_reason, and appends a set_lifecycle entry to the node mutation log. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active).

Transition table (MUST; any request outside it is rejected with lifecycle_transition_invalid/illegal_transition, -32018, error.data.details listing the allowed next states): candidate -> admission_review | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; admission_review -> candidate | admitted | needs_refresh | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; admitted -> needs_refresh | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; needs_refresh -> admitted | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; admission_blocked -> admission_review | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; waiting_activation -> candidate | admission_review | admitted | needs_refresh | admission_blocked | waiting_activation | archived; archived -> candidate | needs_refresh. Self-transitions exist only for the two condition-carrying states (condition update); revival from archived is re-intake (candidate or needs_refresh), never a shortcut back to admitted.

Entry preconditions on stored data (MUST; violated -> lifecycle_transition_invalid/entry_precondition_failed with the failed requirement in error.data.details): candidate requires posterior=null; admitted requires a posterior with status=current AND scoring-eligible close-prior coverage (saturated, or coverage_incomplete with the explicit exploratory waiver, refs present); needs_refresh requires a non-null posterior. Request-shape requirements (MUST; violated -> schema_validation_failed, -32002): admission_blocked and waiting_activation require activation_condition (activation_condition_required) and every other target state must not carry one (activation_condition_unexpected); archived requires a non-empty reason (archived_reason_required). Entering any state that does not carry activation_condition clears the stored condition. Note the normal path INTO admitted is a node.set_posterior write (the engine derives admitted/needs_refresh from the posterior status); explicit set_lifecycle into admitted exists for returns from waiting_activation and equivalent recoveries, and is honest only because the precondition re-checks the stored data.

methods.name: node.set_grounding_audit
methods.summary: Record an independently produced grounding-audit result for a node.

params.name: grounding_audit

status.description: Outcome of the independent grounding check. Honest fail/partial results are recordable; node.promote gates on pass.

report_ref.description: Portable reference to the independently produced grounding record (e.g. a claim_grounding_report artifact) this audit result summarizes. The engine stores the declared reference; content verification stays with project-side audits.

params.description: The grounding-audit result to record. The engine stamps grounding_audit.timestamp itself.

methods.description:
Side-effecting. The ONLY write path for node grounding_audit records: writes the result of an independently produced grounding check (statement-support verification of the idea card's cited claims) onto the node, stamps grounding_audit.timestamp, increments the node revision, and appends a set_grounding_audit entry to the node mutation log. Direct store edits of grounding_audit are not a sanctioned path. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active).

Lifecycle coupling (MUST): the write is legal only while the node's lifecycle_state is admission_review, admitted, or needs_refresh — the same review window as posterior writes: a candidate must first declare admission_review via node.set_lifecycle, and admission_blocked / waiting_activation / archived nodes must be transitioned out first (else lifecycle_transition_invalid/grounding_audit_write_lifecycle_invalid, -32018, with current_state and allowed_states in error.data.details). The write does not derive lifecycle: a failing audit is recorded as data, and any consequent transition (e.g. into admission_blocked with the missing evidence as the activation condition) is an explicit node.set_lifecycle decision.

report_ref (MUST): every record names the independently produced grounding record it summarizes. The engine stores the declared reference verbatim (symmetric with node.set_posterior's survey_ref / close_prior_matrix_ref); a blank (whitespace-only) report_ref is rejected with schema_validation_failed (schema_invalid) even though it satisfies the params minLength, since the field exists to name that record; whether the referenced report actually supports the recorded status stays a project-side audit concern.

methods.name: node.revise_card
methods.summary: Replace a node's complete idea card under optimistic concurrency and return it to scientific re-intake.

params.name: expected_revision
params.description: Exact revision observed by the caller. A mismatch fails with revision_conflict/stale_revision; the engine never merges cards implicitly.

params.name: replacement_idea_card
params.description: Complete replacement validated by the authoritative idea_card_v1 schema. Text and Unicode are stored verbatim.

params.name: reason
params.description: Non-blank explanation of why the scientific proposition changed; recorded verbatim in the append-only event.

methods.description:
Side-effecting. Atomically replaces one complete idea_card after validating it against idea_card_v1 and comparing expected_revision with the current node revision. The before/after card hashes are sha256 over RFC 8785 JCS, so semantically identical object-key reorderings are unchanged while string bytes, including Unicode and whitespace, remain significant. Legal source lifecycle states are candidate, admission_review, admitted, and needs_refresh. admission_blocked, waiting_activation, and archived fail with lifecycle_transition_invalid/idea_card_revision_lifecycle_invalid because a card edit must not silently satisfy an activation or revival condition.

Effects (MUST): increment revision once; set lifecycle_state=candidate and lifecycle_reason=idea_card_revised; clear activation_condition, grounding_audit, posterior, literature_coverage, reduction_report, and reduction_audit; therefore remove the node from current ranking/allocation eligibility. The append-only revision event preserves the exact complete prior node plus explicit prior card, revision, timestamps, lifecycle data, grounding audit, posterior (including any gaia_package_ref), literature coverage, reduction report, and reduction audit. A generated node's current card may retain, falsify, withdraw, or replace the imported novelty hypothesis. The original generation record remains content-pinned in the archived pack; operator_trace carries the current provenance value plus its rewrite history, and the append-only event preserves the prior card and trace. Any retained reserved-prefix claim must descend from the claim assembled at import, remain unique, and keep the closest_prior identity stored in operator_trace; node.revise_card cannot recreate that prefix after withdrawal, and only node.rewrite_provenance may correct the provenance identity.

Idempotency and durability (MUST): payload identity is JCS over all params except idempotency_key. Once a schema-valid campaign_id and non-empty idempotency_key identify a safe store scope and its mutation lock is acquired, the first logical result OR error is committed under that key. Same key + same payload returns or throws that exact stored response without reevaluating later node state; result replays set top-level is_replay=true and create no second event. Same key + any different payload, including one whose card would fail deep schema validation, fails idempotency_key_conflict before that deep validation. Write order for a successful mutation is prepared idempotency record -> atomic latest-node replacement -> fsynced append-only event -> committed idempotency record. A fresh revision preflights the entire existing JSONL ledger and performs no node/event write when it is malformed. Recovery completes only the exact prepared node/event: even when a crash landed only the prepared record, it compares the complete stored before-node, writes the stored resulting node/event without regenerating timestamps, and returns a replay. Interior malformed JSONL and an unrelated malformed final fragment fail closed with internal_error/idea_card_revision_recovery_conflict. A torn final fragment is repaired only when its bytes are a strict prefix of the prepared event. Recovery never appends a missing old event after another same-node event at the target or a later revision.

methods.name: node.rewrite_provenance
methods.summary: Correct a single allowlisted provenance field on a generated node, with the rewrite recorded as engine-owned history.

params.name: field
params.description: The provenance field to rewrite. The allowlist is deliberate: this method is a scoped correction path, not a general node editor. Extending it is a contract change.

params.name: new_value
params.description: The corrected value. For novelty_delta.closest_prior: a survey reference key or a URI of the closest prior work — never a campaign node id.

params.name: reason
params.description: Why this rewrite is a legitimate correction (non-empty; recorded in the engine-owned rewrite history and the node mutation log).

methods.description:
Side-effecting. Scoped provenance-correction path for generated nodes whose recorded provenance violates the contract's documented semantics — currently only operator_trace.inputs.novelty_delta.closest_prior, whose contract requires a URI or survey ref_key of the closest prior work (a campaign node id is not a durable literature reference). The engine rewrites the field, keeps the correction auditable, increments the node revision, and appends a rewrite_provenance entry to the node mutation log. Does not consume step budget. Permitted in any campaign status except completed (campaign_not_active) and in any lifecycle state (a provenance correction is record maintenance, not portfolio progression). The archived generation pack is immutable and retains the original value; the provenance chain is original (pack, content-pinned) -> rewrite history (engine-owned) -> current node value.

Validation (all -32002 schema_validation_failed; error.data.reason values): schema_invalid (new_value has leading/trailing whitespace or is blank, or reason is blank — a closest_prior must be a trimmed URI or survey ref_key and a correction must record why); provenance_field_missing (the node carries no operator_trace.inputs.novelty_delta.closest_prior string — e.g. a seed node; there is nothing to rewrite); rewrite_value_unchanged (new_value equals the stored value — a rewrite must change the record; replays of the SAME correction are handled by idempotency, not by re-execution); closest_prior_node_reference (new_value equals an existing node_id or idea_id IN THIS CAMPAIGN — closest_prior must reference the prior WORK, not a campaign handle, the exact defect this method exists to remove; a non-URI value that is not a handle in this campaign is recorded as a survey ref key and resolved project-side, so a foreign-campaign id or opaque key is a documented project-side-audit boundary, not rejected on shape); evidence_receipt_missing (a URI-shaped new_value — one containing '://' — is absent from the node's operator_trace.evidence_uris_used or has no retrieval receipt in operator_trace.inputs.retrieval_receipts; no retrieval receipt, no URI — same rule as import); delta_claim_missing (the current idea card retains one or more reserved-prefix novelty-delta claims, but they are ambiguous or none uses the closest_prior identity stored in operator_trace; a card with zero reserved claims is valid after reviewed withdrawal and does not trigger this error). A non-URI new_value is recorded as a survey reference key; resolving it against the pinned survey stays a project-side audit concern.

Effects (MUST): (1) operator_trace.inputs.novelty_delta.closest_prior is set to new_value; (2) if the current idea card retains exactly one reserved novelty-delta claim (claim_text prefix 'Novelty delta vs closest prior (<current value>): '), its prefix is synchronized and delta_claim_updated=true; only the old closest_prior URI is replaced or removed from evidence_uris, while reviewer-supplied scientific evidence URIs are preserved; if a reviewed node.revise_card operation already withdrew or replaced that scientific claim, the rewrite does not resurrect it and delta_claim_updated=false; (3) grounding_audit is reset only when the active card claim changed, because only then did the prior audit certify stale claim text; (4) an entry {field, previous_value, new_value, reason, rewritten_at, idempotency_key} is appended to operator_trace.inputs.provenance_rewrites, which is engine-owned (import rejects packs supplying it: trace_key_reserved) and preserves every correction in order; the idempotency_key uniquely identifies the correction for crash recovery.

methods.name: node.import_generated
methods.summary: Import one generation burst (a generation_pack_v1) as derived idea nodes.

params.name: pack
params.description: The burst to import. pack.campaign_id MUST equal the campaign_id param (mismatch is schema_validation_failed/pack_campaign_mismatch).

methods.description:
Side-effecting. The ONLY entry point for derived (non-seed) nodes. The engine: (1) validates the pack against generation_pack_v1 plus the semantic rules below; (2) refuses unless the campaign is running and the batch fits the nodes budget (budget_exhausted with details.exhausted_dimensions=["nodes"] when the batch would exceed max_nodes); (3) mints node_id/idea_id per candidate; (3b) rejects reserved engine-owned trace keys; (4) assembles each idea_card deterministically from card_fields (thesis_statement derived from rationale_draft by the same rule as seed import) and computes operator_trace.params.formalization engine-side (mode explain_then_formalize_deterministic_v1, source_artifact rationale_draft, rationale_hash over the engine's own sanitization) so node.promote can never drift; (5) validates every assembled node against idea_node_v1; (6) archives the pack (verbatim pack plus the engine-assembled nodes) as campaign artifact artifacts/generation/pack-<pack_id>.json and records a portable project://...#sha256:<pack_hash> pack_artifact_ref; the bin/idea-rpc.mjs bridge reads project_root as a top-level stdin envelope field sibling to store_root, not as a method param, so it does not enter idempotency payload hashes; (7) writes nodes, one create node-log entry per node, and recomputed campaign usage (usage.nodes_used = total node count; steps are NOT consumed — import spends the nodes dimension only) under the campaign mutation lock with prepared->committed idempotency. Imported nodes are born with posterior=null and grounding_audit=null — evaluation authority stays with the belief layer; the pack carries no scores.

Semantic validation (all -32002 schema_validation_failed; error.data.reason values): pack_campaign_mismatch (pack.campaign_id != campaign_id param); operator_family_unknown (family outside the committed arity table below); operator_family_not_enabled (family in the table but outside the enabled set: LiteratureMining, FailureRouting — the remaining families are committed vocabulary rejected until their evidence disciplines land in this validator); operator_arity_invalid (parent_node_ids count violates the family's arity, duplicate parents, or AnalogyTransfer without a non-empty rationale_draft.analogy_mapping); trigger_not_enabled (trigger.kind outside the enabled set: manual, survey_updated, failure_recorded; the remaining vocabulary kinds are reserved and rejected until their seam is exercised; non-manual triggers additionally require trigger.artifact_ref); evidence_receipt_missing (an evidence URI in claims, rationale_draft.references, or a URI-shaped novelty_delta.closest_prior lacks a retrieval receipt in trace_inputs.retrieval_receipts [{uri, source}] or is absent from evidence_uris_used); placeholder_evidence_forbidden (the seed placeholder URI appears anywhere in a generated candidate — the scan walks every string in the candidate, including anchors, receipts, references, and novelty fields); reserved_claim_prefix (a supplied card claim begins with the engine-reserved novelty-delta prefix 'Novelty delta vs closest prior (' — the engine alone assembles the sole initial reserved claim at import; a later reviewed revision may retain, falsify, or withdraw it but cannot recreate the reserved prefix after withdrawal); evidence_snapshot_missing (a LiteratureMining candidate in a pack whose evidence_snapshot does not pin survey_artifact_ref + survey_content_hash); anchor_missing (LiteratureMining candidate without trace_inputs.anchor: kind=tension requires statement + non-empty ref_keys; FailureRouting with zero parents requires non-empty trace_inputs.failed_approach_refs, each pinned in evidence_snapshot.failed_approach_refs; FailureRouting with a parent requires that parent to be ARCHIVED — rerouting only makes sense around a node that died); gap_unanchored (LiteratureMining anchor.kind=gap without non-empty anchor.resolved_refs, each receipted); novelty_delta_non_novel (delta_type is parameter_tweak or rewording — declared non-novel by construction); parent_revisions_missing (a candidate's parent id is absent from evidence_snapshot.parent_revisions); parent_revision_invalid (a recorded read-time revision is not one the parent has actually had); intra_pack_duplicate (two candidates in one pack share the same normalized rationale draft — the engine's exact backstop behind the skill-side vector dedup, which must also compare within the burst); dedup_inconsistent (dedup.decision=unique with nearest_similarity >= 0.95, the fixed auto-drop bound); prompt_snapshot_missing (prompt provenance is MANDATORY: every candidate must declare provenance.prompt_snapshot_hash, a pack.prompt_snapshots entry's content must hash to it, every snapshot entry's content must match its own hash, and origin.prompt_hash must equal prompt_snapshot_hash — both hash the same rendered prompt, archived with the pack and verified at import); trace_key_reserved (trace_params carries a formalization key, or trace_inputs carries any of the seven engine-owned keys: trigger, pack_artifact, parent_revisions, target_admission_route, dedup, novelty_delta, provenance_rewrites). Parent nodes must exist in the campaign (node_not_found otherwise). dedup.decision=flagged without override_reason is rejected by the pack schema itself.

Arity table (operator_family -> required parent_node_ids count): Seed -> not importable here (seed import is campaign.init); LiteratureMining -> exactly 0; Mutation -> exactly 1; Recombination -> at least 2; AnalogyTransfer -> 0 or 1 (plus non-empty analogy_mapping); FailureRouting -> 0 or 1 (zero-parent form requires failed_approach_refs).

Crash recovery (import-specific; the generic delete-prepared-and-re-execute path is NOT used when any effect landed): the prepared idempotency record's result payload carries the minted ids, created_at, pack_artifact_ref, pack_hash, and archive_hash. pack_artifact_ref is a project-root-relative project://...#sha256:<pack_hash> reference; pack_hash is the content hash embedded in that ref and archive_hash is sha256 over the ENTIRE archived artifact including the engine-assembled node payloads — recovery verifies both before completing any effect, so completion re-writes only content the original execution produced and validated. On retry, the engine probes all four effect classes — pack artifact resolves with matching pack_hash; every recorded node present with a value-equal IMMUTABLE projection (created_at, lineage, origin, operator_trace, rationale_draft — mutable fields may legitimately have moved); one create node-log entry per node; campaign usage consistent (nodes_used equals the store's node count). Missing effects are COMPLETED from the archived pack artifact — ids are never re-minted and content is never regenerated; only when NOTHING landed (no artifact, no recorded node) does the engine fall back to fresh execution. A value mismatch that cannot be completed refuses with internal_error/import_recovery_conflict for manual repair.

Additional semantics: the pack artifact filename is collision-guarded against artifacts already on disk (an id draw never overwrites an earlier burst's archived audit unit). Recovery completion deliberately does NOT re-check the nodes budget or campaign lifecycle: the burst's budget decision was made at prepare time, so completing an approved burst after an interleaved import may transiently overshoot max_nodes — the recomputed usage makes the overshoot visible and flips a running campaign to exhausted. A mutation attempted while another live process holds the campaign lock fails with internal_error/store_locked (holder pid + lock path in details); locks left by provably dead processes are reclaimed automatically.

methods.name: node.promote
methods.summary: Promote a node to a C2 handoff artifact (idea_card completeness + grounding + posterior gates) within a campaign scope.
methods.description: Side-effecting. Only permitted when campaign status is running. If exhausted: budget_exhausted. Otherwise campaign_not_active. Promotion gates (MUST): the IdeaCard must be schema-valid (including after placeholder evidence URIs are stripped), the formalization trace must be intact, grounding_audit.status must be pass, the node must be in the admitted lifecycle state (else promotion_blocked/node_not_admitted), and the node must carry a non-null posterior (else promotion_blocked/posterior_missing). No numeric posterior threshold is applied: review audits anchors, not scores. Optional method/formalism metadata inside IdeaCard is preserved as run-local context only and MUST NOT be treated as a registry gate. Conditional reduction gate (MUST): if the node has reduction_report != null, reduction_audit must be non-null and reduction_audit.status must be pass; otherwise reject with reduction_audit_failed (-32016) with error.data.reason=reduction_audit_missing or reduction_audit_not_pass.

methods.name: rank.compute
methods.summary: Order campaign nodes by their externally computed belief posterior.
methods.description: Side-effecting (writes a ranking artifact and consumes one step). Only permitted when campaign status is running; otherwise campaign_not_active. Filter is applied within campaign scope. Ranking semantics (MUST): only nodes in the admitted lifecycle state participate in the ordering; every node in any other state MUST be reported explicitly in skipped_nodes with its lifecycle state as the reason (candidate, admission_review, admission_blocked, needs_refresh, waiting_activation, archived) — never silently dropped. Admitted nodes are additionally re-checked against the stored data as defense in depth (relevant for hand-migrated stores): a missing posterior skips with no_posterior; coverage that is not scoring-eligible skips with metadata_only or coverage_incomplete; a posterior whose status is not current skips with posterior_not_current. Remaining nodes are ordered by posterior.value descending; ties break by posterior.evidence_count descending, then by stable input order. An empty ranked_nodes list is a valid result (e.g., when no node has been admitted yet). A node whose stored lifecycle_state is outside the machine (unmigrated store) fails the whole call with lifecycle_transition_invalid/unknown_lifecycle_state (-32018) and a migration hint.
